passages.title: The Reconstruction of Community in Talbot Park, Auckland
passages.source_html:
The Reconstruction of Community in Talbot Park, Auckland 


A 


An architecture of disguise is almost complete at Talbot Park in the heart of Auckland’s Glen Innes. The place was once described as a state housing ghetto, rife with crime, vandalism and other social problems. But today after a $48 million urban renewal makeover, the site is home to 700 residents – 200 more than before – and has people regularly inquiring whether they can buy or rent there. ‘It doesn’t look like social housing,’ Housing New Zealand housing services manager Dene Busby says, ‘apartments and townhouses of the tidy brick and weatherboard would look just as much as home.’ ‘There is no reason why public housing should look cheap in my view,’ says Design Group architect Neil of the eight three-bedroom terrace houses his firm designed. 


B 


Talbot Park is a triangle of government-owned land bounded by Apirana Ave, Pilkington Rd and Point England Rd. in the early 1960s, it was developed for state housing built around a linear park that ran through the middle. Initially, there was a strong sense of a family-friendly community. Former residents recall how the Talbot Park reserve played a big part in their childhoods – a place where the kids in the block came together to play softball, cricket, tiggy, leapfrog and bullrush. Sometimes they’d play ‘Maoris against Pakehas’ but without any animosity. ‘It was all just good fun’, says Georgie Thompson in Ben Schrader’s We Call it Home: A History of State Housing in New Zealand. ‘We had respect for our neighbours and addressed them by title Mr. and Mrs. so-and-so,’ she recalls. 


C 


Quite what went wrong with Talbot Park is not clear. We call it Home Records that the community began to change in the late 1970s as more Pacific Islanders and Europeans moved in. The new arrivals didn’t readily integrate with the community, a ‘them and us’ mentality developed, and residents interact with their neighbours less. What was clear was the buildings were deteriorating and becoming dilapidated, petty crime was on the rise and the reserve – the focus of fond childhood memories – had become a wasteland and was considered unsafe. 


D 


But it wasn’t until 2002 that Housing New Zealand decided the properties needed upgrading. The master renewal plan didn’t take advantage of the maximum accommodation density allowable (one unit per 100 sq metres) but did increase density to one unit per 180 sq m by refurbishing all 108 star flat units, removing the multis and building 111 new home. The Talbot strategy can be summed up as mix, match and manage. Mix up the housing with various plans from a mix of architects, match house styles to what’s built by the private sector, match tenants to the mix, and manage their occupancy. Inevitably cost comes into the equation. ‘If you’re going to build low-cost homes, you’ve got to keep them simple and you can’t afford a fancy bit on them.’ Says Michael Thompson of Architectus which designed the innovative three-level Atrium apartments lining two sides of a covered courtyard. At $300,000 per two-bedroom unit, the building is more expensive but provides for independent disabled accommodation as well as offering solar hot water heating and rainwater collection for toilet cisterns and outside taps.


E 


The renewal project budget at $1.5 million which will provide park pathways, planting, playgrounds, drinking fountains, seating, skateboard rails, a half-size basketball hard court, and a pavilion. But if there was any doubt this is a low socio-economic area, the demographics for the surrounding Tamaki area are sobering. Of the 5000 households there, 55 per cent are statehouses, 28 per cent privately owned (compared to about 65 per cent nationally) and 17 per cent are private rental. The area has a high concentration of households with incomes in the $5000 to $15,000 range and very few with an income of over $70,000. That’s in sharp contrast to the more affluent suburbs like Kohimarama and St John’s that surround the area.


F


‘The design is for people with different culture background,’ says architect James Lunday of Common Ground which designed the 21 large family homes. ‘Architecturally we decided to be relatively conservative – a nice house in its own garden with a bit of space and good indoor-outdoor flow.’ There’s a slight reflection of the whare and a Pacific fale, but not overplayed. ‘The private sector is way behind in urban design and sustainable futures,’ says Bracey, ‘Redesigning streets and parks is a big deal and very difficult to do. The private sector won’t do it, because it’s so hard.’


G 


There’s no doubt good urban design and good architecture play a significant part in the scheme. But probably more important is a new standard of social control. Housing New Zealand calls it ‘intensive tenancy management’. Others view it as social engineering. ‘It’s a model that we are looking at going forward,’ according to Housing New Zealand’s central Auckland regional manager Graham Bodman. ‘The focus is on frequent inspections, helping tenants to get to know each other and trying to create an environment of respect for neighbours,’ says Bodman. That includes some strict rules – no loud parties after 10 pm, no dogs, no cats in the apartments, no washing hung over balcony rails and a requirement to mow lawns and keep the property tidy. Housing New Zealand has also been active in organising morning teas and street barbecues for residents to meet their neighbours. ‘It’s all based on the intensification,’ says Community Renewal project manager Stuart Bracey, ‘We acknowledge if you are going to put more people living closer together, you have to actually help them to live closer together because it creates tension – especially for people that aren’t used to it.’

groups.title: Questions 14-27
groups.source_html:
Questions 14-20 


The passage has seven paragraphs A-G. Choose the correct heading for paragraphs, A-G, from the list below. Write the correct number, i-xi, in boxes 14-20 on your answer sheet.


Options (Word Bank):
A. Financial hardship of community
B. A good tendency of strengthening the supervision
C. Details of plans for the community’s makeover and upgrade
D. Architecture suits families of various ethnic origins
E. Problems arise then the mentality of alienation developed later
F. Introduction of a social housing community with unexpected high standard
G. A practical design and need assist and cooperate in future
H. Closer relationship among neighbors in the original site
I. Different need from a makeup of a low financial background should be considered
J. How to make the community feel safe
K. A plan with details for the house structure
14
正确答案： 14 F. Introduction of a social housing community with unexpected high standard  |  15 H. Closer relationship among neighbors in the original site  |  16 E. Problems arise then the mentality of alienation developed later  |  17 C. Details of plans for the community’s makeover and upgrade  |  18 I. Different need from a makeup of a low financial background should be considered  |  19 G. A practical design and need assist and cooperate in future  |  20 B. A good tendency of strengthening the supervision
Question 14

原文答案句：The place was once described as a state housing ghetto, rife with crime, vandalism and other social problems. But today after a $48 million urban renewal makeover, the site is home to 700 residents – 200 more than before – and has people regularly inquiring whether they can buy or rent there. ‘It doesn’t look like social housing,’ 

对应段落：A段

审题：题目要求为A段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过段落中的关键词如"state housing ghetto"、"urban renewal makeover"、"doesn’t look like social housing"和"public housing should look cheap"可以定位到A段的主题是关于社会住房社区的高标准改造。

解题思路：A段主要描述了Talbot Park地区从过去是犯罪和破坏行为盛行的社会住房贫民窟，到经过4800万美元的城市更新改造后，成为了一个拥有700名居民（比以前多200人）且人们经常询问是否可以购买或租赁。段落中还引用了Housing New Zealand住房服务经理Dene Busby和Design Group建筑师Neil的话，强调了公共住房不应看起来廉价，应该设计得像家一样。因此，选项F"Introduction of a social housing community with unexpected high standard"（介绍了一处超出预期的高标准社会住房社区）最符合A段的内容。


Question 15

原文答案句：Initially, there was a strong sense of a family-friendly community. Former residents recall how the Talbot Park reserve played a big part in their childhoods – a place where the kids in the block came together to play softball, cricket, tiggy, leapfrog and bullrush. Sometimes they’d play ‘Maoris against Pakehas’ but without any animosity. ‘It was all just good fun’, says Georgie Thompson in Ben Schrader’s We Call it Home: A History of State Housing in New Zealand. ‘We had respect for our neighbours and addressed them by title Mr. and Mrs. so-and-so,’ she recalls.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目要求为B段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过阅读B段内容，可以定位到该段主要介绍了Talbot Park的地理位置、早期发展情况以及居民对社区的记忆。

解题思路：根据B段内容，该段主要描述了Talbot Park的地理位置、早期发展情况以及居民对社区的记忆，因此可以推断出该段的标题应该与这些内容相关，早在20世纪60年代初，这片区域就被规划为政府住房区，中央贯穿一条线性公园，那时这里就形成了浓厚的家庭友好的社区氛围，后又举出具体的例子。结合选项，H最符合B段的内容。


Question 16

原文答案句：The community began to change in the late 1970s as more Pacific Islanders and Europeans moved in. The new arrivals didn’t readily integrate with the community, a ‘them and us’ mentality developed, and residents interact with their neighbours less.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目要求为C段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的段落信息，定位到C段的内容，寻找关键信息。

解题思路：C段主要讲述了Talbot Park社区在20世纪70年代末开始发生变化，随着更多太平洋岛国人和欧洲人的迁入，社区中新旧居民之间的融合出现了问题，导致社区互动减少，建筑日渐破败，轻微犯罪案件激增。因此，选择标题E：随后，人们之间日渐疏远，各种问题便接踵而至，能够准确概括C段的主要内容。


Question 17

原文答案句：The master renewal plan didn’t take advantage of the maximum accommodation density allowable (one unit per 100 sq metres) but did increase density to one unit per 180 sq m by refurbishing all 108 star flat units, removing the multis and building 111 new home. The Talbot strategy can be summed up as mix, match and manage.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目要求为D段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：根据D段的第一句话"The master renewal plan didn’t take advantage of the maximum accommodation density allowable..."可以定位到文章中关于改造计划的具体内容。

解题思路：D段主要讲述了Talbot Park的改造计划，包括增加住宅密度、翻新旧建筑、拆除部分建筑和新建房屋等。后文又详细描述改造效果。因此，D段的主题是关于改造计划的详细内容，对应标题为C。


Question 18

原文答案句：But if there was any doubt this is a low socio-economic area, the demographics for the surrounding Tamaki area are sobering. Of the 5000 households there, 55 per cent are statehouses, 28 per cent privately owned (compared to about 65 per cent nationally) and 17 per cent are private rental. The area has a high concentration of households with incomes in the $5000 to $15,000 range and very few with an income of over $70,000. That’s in sharp contrast to the more affluent suburbs like Kohimarama and St John’s that surround the area.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目要求为E段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过阅读E段内容，可以定位到段落主要描述了两个关键信息：一是关于Talbot Park的改造项目预算和公共设施建设（如公园小径、种植、游乐场等），二是该地区的经济状况和人口统计信息（如低收入家庭比例高、与富裕郊区对比等）。

解题思路：E段前半部分详细列举了改造项目中的公共设施建设内容，后半部分则通过人口统计和收入分布数据强调了该地区的低收入特征。因此，该段落的核心内容是要强调，关注低收入群体的需求。在给定的标题选项中，选项I最符合这一主题。


Question 19

原文答案句：The design is for people with different culture background,’ says architect James Lunday of Common Ground which designed the 21 large family homes. ‘Architecturally we decided to be relatively conservative – a nice house in its own garden with a bit of space and good indoor-outdoor flow.’ There’s a slight reflection of the whare and a Pacific fale, but not overplayed.  

对应段落：F段  

审题：题目要求为F段选择一个合适的标题。  

定位关键信息：通过阅读F段，可以发现段落主要讨论的是建筑设计的特点以及为不同文化背景的人设计房屋的理念。  

解题思路：段落F的核心内容包含两个部分：首先，建筑师James Lunday指出，设计是为了适应不同文化背景的人，并采用了相对保守但实用的方案（"a nice house in its own garden with a bit of space and good indoor-outdoor flow"），这体现了 “实用的设计” (A practical design)。接着，Bracey指出私营部门在 urban design and sustainable futures 方面落后，并且不会去承担重新设计街道和公园这类困难的任务，这暗示了此类项目 “未来需要（政府或公共部门的）援助和合作” (need assist and cooperate in future)。因此，段落F的核心是阐述当前的设计理念及其对未来合作的需求。因此，F段对应的标题是G选项。


Question 20

原文答案句：There’s no doubt good urban design and good architecture play a significant part in the scheme. But probably more important is a new standard of social control. Housing New Zealand calls it ‘intensive tenancy management’.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目要求为G段选择一个合适的标题。

定位关键信息：通过阅读G段，可以发现段落主要讨论的是社区管理，包括“intensive tenancy management”和“social engineering”等概念。

解题思路：G段开头提到“good urban design and good architecture play a significant part in the scheme”，优质的城镇设计和建筑毫无疑问扮演着重要的角色，但接着强调“more important is a new standard of social control”，更重要的是建立社区管控的新标准，并详细介绍了Housing New Zealand的“intensive tenancy management”措施，如频繁检查、帮助租户相互认识、创造尊重邻居的环境等。这些内容与选项B吻合，因此选择选项B作为G段的标题。


Questions 21-23 


Use the information in the passage to match the people (listed A-E) with opinions or deeds below. Write the appropriate letters, A-E, in boxes 21-23 on your answer sheet.


List of people 

A Michael Thompson 

B Graham Bodman 

C Stuart Bracey 

D James Lunday 

E Dene Busby 

 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
21

21 Design should meet the need of mix-raced cultural background.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：‘The design is for people with different culture background,’ says architect James Lunday of Common Ground which designed the 21 large family homes.

对应段落：F段  

审题：题目问的是谁认为设计应该满足不同种族文化背景的需求。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"mix-raced cultural background"（不同种族文化背景）定位答案。在F段中，James Lunday明确提到"The design is for people with different culture background"，与题目信息完全匹配。  

解题思路：原文中James Lunday直接表达了设计要满足不同文化背景人群的需求，因此D选项（James Lunday）是正确答案。
22

22 For a better living environment, regulations and social control should be imperative. 
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：‘The focus is on frequent inspections, helping tenants to get to know each other and trying to create an environment of respect for neighbours,’ says Bodman. That includes some strict rules – no loud parties after 10 pm, no dogs, no cats in the apartments, no washing hung over balcony rails and a requirement to mow lawns and keep the property tidy.  

对应段落：G段  

审题：题目问的是“为了更好的生活环境，有必要设立规章制度，进行社会管控”，需要找到相关的人物及其观点。  

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是“regulations and social control”，通过这些词可以定位到G段中Bodman提到的严格规则和社会管控措施。  

解题思路：G段中Bodman明确提到了严格的规章制度，如“晚上10点后禁止举办喧闹聚会、禁止养犬猫、禁止在阳台栏杆上晾晒衣物，同时要求定期修剪草坪并保持社区整洁。这些内容直接对应题目中的“regulations and social control”。此外，Bodman还提到，重点在于加强日常巡查、促进住户相互了解，并努力营造邻里和睦的氛围，这与题目中的“better living environment”和“imperative”相吻合。因此，Bodman的观点和行为与题目要求一致，答案为B。
23

23 Organising more community’s activities helps to strengthen the relationship in the community.
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Housing New Zealand has also been active in organising morning teas and street barbecues for residents to meet their neighbours. ‘It’s all based on the intensification,’ says Community Renewal project manager Stuart Bracey, ‘We acknowledge if you are going to put more people living closer together, you have to actually help them to live closer together because it creates tension – especially for people that aren’t used to it.’

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是“组织更多的社区活动有助于加强社区关系”，需要找到支持这一观点的人物。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“Organising more community’s activities”和“strengthen the relationship in the community”来定位答案。在G段中找到“organising morning teas and street barbecues”这一组织社区活动的描述。

解题思路：原文中提到Housing New Zealand积极组织晨茶和街头烧烤活动，目的是让居民互相认识。这与题目中“组织更多社区活动有助于加强社区关系”的意思一致，因此G段是答案所在段落。根据题目要求匹配人物，G段提到的相关人物是Housing New Zealand的central Auckland regional manager Graham Bodman，对应选项C。

Questions 24-27 


Complete the summary below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 24-27 on your answer sheet.


24

In the year 2002, the Talbot decided to raise housing standard, yet the plan was to build homes go much beyond the accommodation limit and people complain about the high living 24  .  

正确答案： 24 density
Question 24

原文答案句：The master renewal plan didn’t take advantage of the maximum accommodation density allowable (one unit per 100 sq metres) but did increase density to one unit per 180 sq m by refurbishing all 108 star flat units, removing the multis and building 111 new home.  

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问的是2002年塔尔博特社区决定提高住房标准，然而建房计划却远超居住容量限制，人们抱怨过高的什么。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“2002”、“raise housing standard”、“build homes”、“accommodation limit”和“complain about the high living”定位到D段，该段提到2002年住房新西兰决定升级房产，并描述了改造计划的具体措施，包括增加密度。  

解题思路：原文中明确提到“increase density”（增加密度）。由于原文提到“increase density”且题目提到“high living”，因此“density”是唯一符合语境的答案。


25

And as the various plans were complemented under the designs of many 25 together, made house styles go with the part designed by individuals, matched tenants from a different culture.

正确答案： 25 architects
Question 25

原文答案句：  

The Talbot strategy can be summed up as mix, match and manage. Mix up the housing with various plans from a mix of architects, match house styles to what’s built by the private sector, match tenants to the mix, and manage their occupancy.  

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问的是“在塔博特公园的重建过程中，由谁将多种方案融合在一起，使建筑风格与个人设计的元素相得益彰，并为不同文化的租户提供了契合的居住空间”。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“various plans were complemented under the designs of many”定位到D段中的“Mix up the housing with various plans from a mix of architects”。  

解题思路：根据原文句子“Mix up the housing with various plans from a mix of architects”，将建筑师设计的多种方案混合搭配，可以确定“various plans”是由“a mix of architects”共同设计的，因此答案为“architects”。题目中的“many”对应原文中的“a mix of”，说明是由多位专业人士共同设计，结合上下文可知这些专业人士是“architects”。


26

As for the finance, reconstruction program’s major concern is to build a house within low 26 ;

正确答案： 26 budget
Question 26

原文答案句：‘If you’re going to build low-cost homes, you’ve got to keep them simple and you can’t afford a fancy bit on them.’ Says Michael Thompson of Architectus which designed the innovative three-level Atrium apartments lining two sides of a covered courtyard. 

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是关于改造项目的财务方面，需要注意的是，要以什么来建造房屋。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"finance"和"reconstruction program"可以定位到D段，该段提到了改造项目的财务方面。原文中提到"If you’re going to build low-cost homes, you’ve got to keep them simple and you can’t afford a fancy bit on them."这句话表明，在建造低成本房屋时，需要保持简单，不能有奢华的部分。因此，可以确定答案与"cost"相关。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子"If you’re going to build low-cost homes, you’ve got to keep them simple and you can’t afford a fancy bit on them."可以确定，改造项目的财务方面主要关注的是以低成本来建造房屋。因此，答案为"budget"。


27

finally, just as expert predicted, residents will agree on building a relatively conventional house in its own 27 , which provides considerable space to move around.

正确答案： 27 garden
Question 27

原文答案句：There’s a nice house in its own garden with a bit of space and good indoor-outdoor flow.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是居民最终同意建造一个相对常规的房子，这个房子有自己的什么，并且提供了相当大的活动空间。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的"building a relatively conventional house in its own"和"provides considerable space to move around"可以定位到F段中的"nice house in its own garden with a bit of space"。

解题思路：根据原文句子，在建筑设计上，他们选择保持相对常规的布局，在专属花园里打造一座雅致住宅，既留有充足空间，又实现室内外空间的自然衔接。房子有自己的"garden"，并且有"space"，与题目中的描述相符，因此答案为"garden"。
